Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed nominal phrase (NOUN + stative predicate), metaphorical equative construction.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To be deeply worried or mentally burdened, as if the heart is heavy.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain Rephrasi: To feel very worried or anxious.
Usage Contexts: Daily speech, family conversations, reflective storytelling.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects traditional Chukchi belief associating the heart (ӄэйвылгын) with emotional strength and spirit.
First Example Sentence: Гымнан ӄэйвылгын ӄытрын, ытлык ымыӈы рыткын.
Second Example Sentence: Ӄорак ӄэйвылгын ӄытрын ынэн, этлыӈ ырыкын.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Plural noun + reflexive verb form; metaphorical reflexive structure.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Used in coastal communities referring to stirring snow or sea foam meaninglessly.
First Example Sentence: Гыт Ӄоракыт ӄэвыргын, мури ытлык рыткын.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Stative verb + temporal noun phrase; fixed descriptive clause.
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To wait in vain for something unlikely.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain Rephrasi: To expect something that will not happen.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Derived from imagery of watching endless tundra winds without result.
Heart imagery linked to courage in reindeer-herding traditions.
